# Recommended Briefing Agenda: Pepper Minton

## SnackStack Technologies | Full-Day Executive Briefing

---

**Visitor:** Pepper Minton, CTO, SnackStack Technologies
**VVIP Tier:** Gold
**Date:** TBD (pending scheduling)
**Location:** Raleigh Executive Briefing Center -- Developer Collaboration Room (whiteboard-first setup)
**Executive Sponsor:** Carlos Diaz, VP Developer Experience
**Briefing Coordinator:** EBC Operations Team

---

### Agenda Overview

This agenda is structured around Pepper's engineering-first communication style and the post-incident priority to rebuild confidence in auto-scaling. The day opens with a candid incident retrospective (no spin) and transitions into hands-on whiteboard work on scaling and edge computing. ZERO marketing slides across the entire day. Every session is whiteboard-first, demo-second, slides never. Time blocks are intentionally short (25-45 min) to match her startup-paced attention rhythm.

### Session Details

#### 9:00 AM -- Welcome & Austin-Style Breakfast Tacos (30 min)

**Format:** Informal welcome, casual seating
**Protocol:** Gold VVIP -- EBC Manager greets at lobby and escorts to Developer Collaboration Room
**Catering:** Breakfast tacos (because Austin), oat milk lattes, cold brew, seasonal fruit
**Setup:** Whiteboards on wheels, fresh markers (dry erase AND wet erase), no projector warmed up

**Talking points for welcome:**
- Acknowledge her 100% SAB attendance and the public case study her team authored
- Light conversation: Austin weather, her team's engineering culture
- Set expectations: "No slides today. Just whiteboards, demos, and honest conversation."

> **Note:** Pepper is ex-startup founder. Skip formal introductions. She will remember everyone's name after one intro.

---

#### 9:30 AM -- Viral Incident Retrospective (60 min)

**Format:** Candid engineering post-mortem discussion, whiteboard-driven
**Presenter:** Engineering SRE Lead + Platform Product Lead
**Priority:** CRITICAL -- this session sets the tone for the entire day

**Objective:** Walk through the March 14 incident with full honesty. No corporate language, no softening. Pepper's team already knows what happened -- our job is to demonstrate that we know too, and that we have learned.

**Content to cover:**
- Full incident timeline (minute-by-minute)
- Root cause analysis: HPA threshold configuration + cluster headroom
- What should have worked but did not
- What our internal post-mortem identified as process gaps
- Concrete scaling improvements shipped in the last 30 days
- How we will prevent similar incidents in future customer deployments

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Bring the actual on-call engineer who led the incident response
- [ ] Print the incident timeline and tape it to the wall for reference
- [ ] Have the real RCA document, not a sanitized version
- [ ] NO SLIDES. Walk through it on a whiteboard.

> **Caution:** Pepper will ask hard questions about why auto-scaling failed. Do not deflect. If we do not know something, say we do not know.

---

#### 10:30 AM -- Auto-Scaling Architecture Whiteboard (90 min)

**Format:** Interactive whiteboard architecture session
**Presenter:** Platform Engineering Team
**Tie to pipeline:** Directly supports the $480K Auto-Scaling Advanced Tier opportunity

**Objective:** Co-design the reference scaling architecture for viral traffic surges. This is not a presentation -- it is a working session where Pepper helps shape the architecture alongside our engineers.

**Content to cover:**
- Multi-layer scaling strategy (HPA + VPA + Cluster Autoscaler + Custom Metrics)
- Predictive scaling using traffic pattern ML
- Warm pool strategies for sub-60-second response
- Cost/performance tradeoffs at SnackStack's scale
- Integration with SnackStack's existing TikTok traffic monitoring

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Two whiteboards side-by-side (one for current state, one for target state)
- [ ] Bring the engineer who wrote the HPA v2 controller
- [ ] Have sample YAML configurations ready as handouts
- [ ] Standing desk options available -- Pepper prefers standing for working sessions

> **Opportunity:** If this session goes well, offer a dedicated 2-day scaling workshop with Pepper's platform team at our Austin office. No cost. Pure goodwill.

---

#### 12:00 PM -- Working Lunch: Tacos + Architecture Talk (60 min)

**Format:** Informal working lunch, continued whiteboard discussion
**Catering:** Full taco spread (breakfast menu again, because tacos), horchata, agua fresca
**Presenter:** Account Team + Engineering

**Objective:** Keep the momentum from the morning whiteboard session. Allow architectural discussion to continue over food. Not a presentation.

**Content to cover:**
- Follow-up questions from morning session
- SnackStack's platform team structure and hiring plans
- Discussion of the workshop offer (if raised in morning)

> **Note:** Pepper dislikes formal sit-down meals during briefings. Buffet-style, casual seating. No toasts or speeches.

---

#### 1:00 PM -- Smart Kitchen Edge Computing Deep-Dive (90 min)

**Format:** Architecture workshop with live demo component
**Presenter:** Edge Computing Engineering Lead
**Tie to pipeline:** Supports the $1.8M Smart Kitchen Edge Platform opportunity

**Objective:** This is the future ARR story. SnackStack is launching connected smart kitchen devices in Q4 2026 and needs an edge platform. Demonstrate that OpenShift for edge is purpose-built for this use case.

**Content to cover:**
- Edge computing reference architecture for consumer IoT devices
- On-device ML inference for recipe recommendations
- OTA update strategy for 1M+ deployed devices
- Telemetry and observability at edge scale
- Competitive positioning vs. AWS IoT Greengrass (Pepper will ask)

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Bring a physical smart device running OpenShift at edge (live demo)
- [ ] Architecture whiteboard with device fleet topology
- [ ] Honest comparison to AWS IoT Greengrass -- strengths and weaknesses of both
- [ ] Engineering contact from an existing edge customer for reference

---

#### 2:30 PM -- Hands-on Lab: Chaos Engineering (90 min)

**Format:** Interactive hands-on chaos engineering lab
**Facilitator:** Senior Solutions Architect
**Setup:** Pre-configured OpenShift cluster with sample workload

**Objective:** Pepper will test things live. Give her a playground where she can actually break things and see the platform recover.

**Lab exercises:**
1. Deploy a sample recipe-engine-like workload (10 min)
2. Inject pod failures, node failures, network partitions (25 min)
3. Trigger a synthetic traffic spike and watch auto-scaling respond (20 min)
4. Configure custom metrics-based scaling (20 min)
5. Open exploration -- let Pepper break whatever she wants (15 min)

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Pre-configured cluster with chaos engineering tools installed
- [ ] Backup cluster ready in case Pepper breaks the primary one
- [ ] Standing lab station available
- [ ] Solutions Architect who can think on their feet

> **Note:** Pepper will intentionally try to break things. This is a good thing. Encourage it.

---

#### 4:00 PM -- Sponsor Drop-in: Carlos Diaz (30 min)

**Format:** Coffee meeting, peer-to-peer engineering chat
**Attendees:** Pepper Minton, Carlos Diaz, Account Lead
**Protocol:** Gold VVIP -- sponsor drop-in required

**Talking points for Carlos Diaz:**
1. Open with engineering culture and recent incidents (he will relate -- ex-SRE himself)
2. Acknowledge the case study her team published and thank her for the advocacy
3. Discuss the upcoming SAB topic on developer experience and platform engineering
4. Light touch on renewal timeline and partnership trajectory
5. Offer to personally connect her with other CTOs in the SAB community

> **Prep for Carlos:** He and Pepper have similar engineering backgrounds. Let the conversation go technical. Do not bring slides to this meeting.

---

#### 4:30 PM -- Wrap-up & Next Steps (30 min)

**Format:** Quick, structured close-out (no long speeches)
**Presenter:** Account Lead

**Close-out checklist:**
- [ ] Summarize commitments made during the day
- [ ] Confirm workshop offer with target dates
- [ ] Confirm edge computing follow-up architecture review
- [ ] Collect briefing feedback (quick verbal read, formal NPS later)
- [ ] Gift presentation (something engineering-themed, NOT branded swag)

**Commitments to confirm:**
1. Auto-scaling architecture review document delivery (close the overdue SAB item)
2. 2-day scaling workshop in Austin -- scheduling within 2 weeks
3. Smart kitchen edge computing proposal -- timeline to June decision
4. Next SAB meeting topic input from Pepper
